sourceMovie.title: Hocus Pocus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hocus Pocus (1993) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kenny Ortega's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Onward
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hocus Pocus clicked for you, Onward (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hocus Pocus?
faq.answer: Try Onward, Casper, Slumberland—each shares DNA with Hocus Pocus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hocus Pocus?
faq.answer: Hocus Pocus (1993) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hocus Pocus part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hocus Pocus Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
